=== L080439
.GLOSS	mountain
.MEANING	mountain
.FORM	asipikwi
.FORM	asipiki
.KANA	アシヒキ
.KANA	アシヒキ
.POS	noun
.NOTE	Meaning/usage conventionalized from asipik(w)i no, MK for mountains and the like
.NOTE	The older form is presumably "asipikwi"
.RELATED	ref_target=L050860	asipikwi
.RELATED	ref_target=L091054	asipikwino

---------------------------------------------------
=== L050860
.GLOSS	UNKNOWN
.MEANING	[unknown, only found in the MK asipik(w)ino]
.FORM	asipikwi
.FORM	asipiki
.KANA	アシヒキ
.KANA	アシヒキ
.POS	noun
.NOTE	The older form is presumably "asipikwi"; "asipiki" is written in kungana and only attested in the later volumes (JDB). Usually analysed as asi-pikwi, with pikwi perhaps related to pik- ‘pull’ or piku ‘low’. It has been suggested that asi is the “foot” of a mountain, giving “low-footed (mountains)” (Vovin) or “(mountains whose) feet drag on” but the foot of a mountain is suswo ‘hem’ or yamasita in OJ. Alternatively, “making one’s feet drag” or “foot-wearying” (Horton) is supported by the attestation (albeit logographic) of the phrase asi piku ‘having a limp (< “dragging one’s leg”)’ in MYS.2.128 and the logographic rendering of asipikwi as 足引, though this may reflect a folk etymology applied after the original meaning had been lost, and the shape of pikwi would have to correspond to a hypothetical UB verb rather than (QD) pik- itself.
.RELATED	ref_target=L091054	asipikwino
.RELATED	ref_target=L080439	asipikwi

Unapi river may be present-day Unami river (宇波川), Himi city, Toyama Prefecture (Nakanishi 1985: 430, Hashimoto 1985: 218) but Omodaka (1984.17: 164-165) doubts this (Vovin 2015: 149).

=== L060017
.GLOSS	Ami-no-ura; Aminoura Bay
.MEANING	Ami-no-ura; Aminoura Bay
.FORM	aminoura
.KANA	アミノウラ
.POS	place name
.NOTE	Ami-no-ura in MYS.1.5 is the shore of Sakaide-shi (坂出市) in Kagawa Prefecture, Shikoku (Nakanishi 1985: 420 in Vovin 2017: 28). Ami-no-ura in MYS.1.40 is probably the Obama (小浜) seashore in Mie prefecture facing Tōshi-jima (答志島) (Vovin 2017: 114). The location of Ami-no-ura in MYS.15.3610b is not clear (Nakanishi 1985: 420 in Vovin 2009: 70).

.NOTE	Alternative interpretation is "far from heaven" but I think that makes less sense; prenasalisation likely the result of an intervening "ni" or "no" in the original expression

.COMPOUND	ref_target=L030770a	sakari
.RELATED	ref_target=L050089	pina
